paper.title: 한국어 리뷰 에이전트의 증거 계층 분해에 관한 연구

claims.text: The authors propose a review agent/pipeline that decomposes each review criticism into three evidence tiers: grounded, weak, and out-of-scope, by linking criticisms to specific claims in the paper.
claims.rationale: The abstract and methods explicitly state that they built a pipeline that classifies each criticism into three tiers based on token-overlap matching between criticisms and claims/evidence text, so this claim is directly supported by descriptions of the system.

claims.text: On five labeled fixed cases, the proposed system achieved tier accuracy 1.0, target-claim accuracy 0.93, and an out-of-scope capture rate of 1.0.
claims.rationale: The experimental section reports evaluation on five labeled fixed cases and provides the exact performance numbers for tier accuracy, target-claim accuracy, and out-of-scope capture rate, directly supporting this claim.

claims.text: The next-experiment selector that recommends a single follow-up experiment for the authors attains 60 percentage points higher precision than an existing method (on their fixed cases).
claims.rationale: The abstract states that the selector achieves 60%p higher precision than the existing approach; while the scope is limited to their fixed cases, it is a directly reported comparative result.

claims.text: Decomposing reviews into evidence tiers helps authors trace the basis of criticisms and increases the precision of next-experiment selection.
claims.rationale: The conclusion asserts benefits for authors (better tracing of criticism basis and higher next-experiment precision). The precision improvement is numerically supported in the abstract, but there is no direct user study or explicit measurement of authors’ ability to trace criticisms, so the overall causal claim about helping authors is only weakly supported.

claims.text: A large-scale validation on a real review corpus has not yet been conducted and remains for future work.
claims.rationale: The experimental section explicitly notes that evaluation was only on five labeled fixed cases and that large-scale real-review-corpus validation has not yet been performed, making this limitation/future-work claim directly supported.

claims.text: The authors plan to extend their approach to a mixed Korean-English review corpus in future work.
claims.rationale: The conclusion describes this as a future plan rather than completed work; it will require further experimentation, so it is prospective and thus marked as needing experiment.

evidence.text: 우리는 리뷰의 각 비판을 근거 있음, 약함, 범위 밖의 세 계층으로 분류하는 파이프라인을 구축했다.
다섯 개의 고정 사례에서 계층 정확도 1.0을 달성했으며, 저자에게 단 하나의 다음 실험을 제안하는 선택기가 기존 방식보다 60%p 높은 정밀도를 보였다.
다섯 개의 라벨링된 고정 사례에서 평가했다.
리뷰를 증거 계층으로 분해하면 저자가 비판의 근거를 추적할 수 있고, 다음 실험 선택의 정밀도가 올라간다.
분류기는 토큰 중첩 기반 매칭으로 비판과 주장을 연결한다.
다만 대규모 실제 리뷰 코퍼스에서의 검증은 아직 수행하지 못했으며, 이는 후속 연구로 남긴다.
향후 한국어와 영어가 혼합된 리뷰 코퍼스로 확장할 계획이다.

limitations.text: 다만 대규모 실제 리뷰 코퍼스에서의 검증은 아직 수행하지 못했으며, 이는 후속 연구로 남긴다.

generated_criticisms.text: The claim that decomposing reviews into evidence tiers helps authors trace the basis of criticisms and improves next-experiment selection is presented as a conclusion rather than being empirically demonstrated with user studies or explicit behavioral metrics, so the causal effect on authors remains speculative.
generated_criticisms.quote: 리뷰를 증거 계층으로 분해하면 저자가 비판의 근거를 추적할 수 있고, 다음 실험 선택의 정밀도가 올라간다.
generated_criticisms.hidden_assumption: Stating that authors "can" trace criticism bases and that precision "goes up" implies a demonstrated causal effect on real users, even though no user-centric evaluation or comparison on actual author workflows is reported.

generated_criticisms.text: Relying solely on token-overlap-based matching to link criticisms to claims may be brittle to paraphrasing and more abstract critiques, potentially limiting the robustness of the proposed three-tier decomposition in realistic settings.
generated_criticisms.quote: 분류기는 토큰 중첩 기반 매칭으로 비판과 주장을 연결한다.
generated_criticisms.hidden_assumption: Token overlap alone is sufficient to robustly capture the semantic connection between criticisms and underlying claims across the variety of linguistic expressions found in reviews.

filtered_criticisms.text: The strong reported accuracies may not generalize because they are obtained only on five labeled fixed cases, which is a very limited evaluation setting for such a system.
filtered_criticisms.quote: 다섯 개의 라벨링된 고정 사례에서 평가했다.
filtered_criticisms.hidden_assumption: Performance measured on a very small, fixed set of five labeled cases is representative of performance on the broader and more diverse space of real peer reviews.
filtered_criticisms.filter_reason: off_scope: This criticism primarily restates a limitation that the authors already acknowledge without adding a concrete additional check. The paper explicitly says: "다섯 개의 라벨링된 고정 사례에서 평가했다." and further notes that "대규모 실제 리뷰 코퍼스에서의 검증은 아직 수행하지 못했으며, 이는 후속 연구로 남긴다." The concern about generalization from five fixed cases is therefore already flagged by the authors themselves, and the comment does not propose a specific new evaluation or analysis beyond that.

filtered_criticisms.text: The plan to extend the approach to mixed Korean-English review corpora is non-trivial and will require careful experiments to verify that the current token-overlap pipeline and tiering scheme still work under code-switching and cross-lingual variability.
filtered_criticisms.quote: 향후 한국어와 영어가 혼합된 리뷰 코퍼스로 확장할 계획이다.
filtered_criticisms.hidden_assumption: The current methods can be straightforwardly extended to mixed-language data without substantial redesign or empirical validation of cross-lingual behavior.
filtered_criticisms.filter_reason: off_scope: Out of scope as a basis for criticism. The quoted passage is explicitly a future plan: "향후 한국어와 영어가 혼합된 리뷰 코퍼스로 확장할 계획이다." The paper does not claim that the current token-overlap pipeline already works on mixed Korean-English corpora, only that they intend to extend it. Asking for detailed cross-lingual experiments for a stated future plan goes beyond what the paper sets out to accomplish in its current scope, so this should not be treated as a present-tense weakness of the work.

selected_next_experiment.hypothesis: A bounded experiment can resolve whether the paper's evidence supports C4: Decomposing reviews into evidence tiers helps authors trace the basis of criticisms and increases the precision of next-experiment selection.
selected_next_experiment.baseline: Use the closest reported evidence sentence as the baseline before running the missing check.
selected_next_experiment.keep_condition: claim-specific pass rate passes on a held-out fixture and the evidence can be reproduced from saved artifacts.
selected_next_experiment.discard_condition: claim-specific pass rate fails on the held-out fixture or requires evidence not present in the paper artifacts.
selected_next_experiment.verify: Run the smallest saved fixture that directly targets C4 and attach the run artifact.
